title: Design URL Shortener (TinyURL)
description: Design a scalable URL shortening service like TinyURL or Bitly. Handle billions of URLs, high read volume, and analytics.
hints: Functional Requirements: shorten URL, redirect, custom alias, expiry, analytics.
Non-Functional: High availability, low latency reads, 100:1 read-to-write ratio.
Encoding: Base62 encoding of an auto-incrementing ID. 7-char string supports 3.5 trillion URLs.
Key Generation Service (KGS): Pre-generate unique keys offline to avoid collision.
Storage: NoSQL (DynamoDB/Cassandra) for key-value lookups. Cache hot URLs with Redis.

title: Design WhatsApp / Messaging System
description: Design a real-time messaging platform supporting one-on-one chats, group chats, media sharing, read receipts, and online status.
hints: Real-time communication via WebSocket connections. Fallback to long-polling.
Message flow: Sender -> Chat Server -> if recipient online, push via WebSocket. Else, store and forward.
Message storage: Cassandra (write-heavy, partition by chat_id). Message queue (Kafka) for async delivery.
Group chats: Fan-out on write (push to each member's queue) or fan-out on read (pull from group queue).
End-to-End Encryption: Signal Protocol. Keys exchanged during handshake. Server cannot read messages.

title: Design Netflix / Video Streaming
description: Design a global video streaming service that serves millions of concurrent users with low latency.
hints: Video upload pipeline: Upload to S3 -> Transcoding service (FFmpeg) -> Multiple resolutions/codecs -> CDN distribution.
Content Delivery: Use CDN (CloudFront, Akamai) with edge caching. Geo-replicate popular content.
Adaptive Bitrate Streaming (ABR): Client adjusts quality based on bandwidth (HLS, DASH protocols).
Recommendation Engine: Collaborative filtering + Content-based filtering. ML pipeline for personalization.
Microservices: User Service, Video Service, Search Service, Recommendation Service, Billing Service.

title: Design Uber / Ride Sharing
description: Design a ride-sharing platform supporting real-time matching, location tracking, fare estimation, and payments.
hints: Location tracking: Drivers send GPS updates every 3-5 seconds. Store in-memory with QuadTree or GeoHash.
Matching: Find nearby drivers using spatial indexing. Rank by ETA, rating, vehicle type.
Supply Service stores driver locations. Demand Service stores ride requests. Match Service connects both.
Fare calculation: Base fare + time + distance + surge pricing (dynamic based on demand/supply ratio).
Scale: Use Kafka for event streaming, Redis for caching, PostGIS or H3 for geo-spatial queries.

title: Design YouTube
description: Design a video-sharing platform supporting video upload, transcoding, search, recommendations, and streaming.
hints: Similar to Netflix but with user-generated content. Upload pipeline needs moderation.
Thumbnail generation during transcoding. Store metadata in SQL, video chunks in blob storage.
Search: Elasticsearch for full-text search on titles, descriptions, tags.
View count: Use eventual consistency. Aggregate counts asynchronously via Kafka + real-time counters.
Comments: Store in a tree structure. Use denormalization for read performance.

title: Design Twitter
description: Design a social media platform supporting posting tweets, following users, timeline generation, and trending topics.
hints: Timeline generation: Fan-out on write (precompute timelines for followers) vs. Fan-out on read (pull at read time).
Hybrid approach: Fan-out on write for normal users, fan-out on read for celebrity users (high follower count).
Tweet storage: SQL for metadata, Redis for pre-computed timelines, CDN for media.
Search & Trending: Use Elasticsearch for search. Count hashtags in sliding windows for trending.
Scale: Partition tweets by user_id. Cache hot timelines. Use Kafka for async timeline fan-out.

title: Design Amazon E-Commerce (HLD)
description: Design a large-scale e-commerce platform handling product catalog, search, cart, checkout, payments, and logistics.
hints: Microservices: Product Service, User Service, Cart Service, Order Service, Payment Service, Inventory Service, Notification Service.
Product search: Elasticsearch with faceted search (filters by price, brand, rating).
Inventory: Distributed inventory with reservation model. Decrement on order, increment on cancellation.
Flash Sales / Black Friday: Rate limiting, queue-based checkout, pre-warm caches, auto-scaling.
Payment: Use Saga pattern for distributed transactions. Compensate on failure.

title: Design Walmart Inventory Management System
description: Design a scalable inventory management system for a global retailer like Walmart. Handle real-time stock updates, warehouse management, and supply chain coordination.
hints: Requirements: Real-time stock visibility, multi-warehouse support, low-stock alerts, demand forecasting.
Data model: SKU-based inventory. Track quantity per (SKU, warehouse, location). Event-sourced updates.
Consistency: Strong consistency for stock decrements (avoid overselling). Use distributed locks or DB-level CAS.
Event-Driven: Use Kafka for inventory events (stock received, stock sold, stock transferred). CQRS for read/write separation.
Scale: Partition by warehouse/region. Cache hot SKUs in Redis. Batch sync with supplier systems.

title: Design Notification System
description: Design a notification service that supports push notifications, SMS, email, and in-app notifications at scale.
hints: Components: API Gateway, Notification Service, Message Queue, Workers (per channel), Template Engine.
Channels: Push (Firebase/APNS), SMS (Twilio), Email (SES), In-App (WebSocket).
Use message queues (Kafka/SQS) for reliability and retry. Dead letter queue for failed notifications.
Rate limiting per user to prevent spam. Priority queues for urgent vs. marketing notifications.
Deduplication: Use notification_id + user_id as idempotency key.

title: Design Payment System
description: Design a payment processing system that handles transactions, refunds, settlements, and fraud detection.
hints: Requirements: Process payments, handle refunds, support multiple payment methods, detect fraud.
Flow: Order Service -> Payment Service -> Payment Gateway (Stripe/PayPal) -> Bank.
Idempotency: Use idempotency keys to prevent double charges. Store transaction state machine.
Saga Pattern: For distributed transactions across Order, Payment, and Inventory services.
Fraud Detection: ML-based scoring. Flag suspicious transactions for manual review.

title: Design Search Autocomplete / Typeahead
description: Design a search autocomplete system that provides real-time suggestions as the user types.
hints: Use a Trie data structure for prefix-based lookups.
Store frequency counts at each node. Return top-k suggestions.
Cache popular prefixes in Redis for low latency.
Update frequencies asynchronously via a batch pipeline (MapReduce).
Shard the Trie by first character or hash prefix for horizontal scaling.

title: Design Distributed Cache (Redis)
description: Design a distributed in-memory caching system like Redis or Memcached. Handle replication, partitioning, and eviction.
hints: Partitioning: Consistent hashing to distribute keys across nodes. Virtual nodes for balance.
Eviction policies: LRU, LFU, TTL-based. Approximate LRU with sampling for performance.
Replication: Master-Slave replication for read scaling. Async replication with eventual consistency.
Failure handling: Sentinel for automatic failover. Gossip protocol for cluster health.
Client-side: Use client-side caching with cache invalidation messages.
